bullets.text: A suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, MV Hondius, has led to the deaths of three passengers, with the WHO confirming two cases and three additional people falling ill, including a British crew member in need of urgent medical care.

Russian attacks have killed over 20 people ahead of proposed ceasefires from both Kyiv and Moscow; Kyiv announced a truce starting May 6, intending to "act symmetrically" after Moscow's declared pause for its Victory Day parade.
Nine coal miners died in a gas explosion in Sutatausa, Colombia, marking the latest deadly incident in the region, which experienced another fatal mine disaster in 2023.
The family of a five-year-old girl who died after going missing from an Alice Springs town camp last month say they feel let down by the system after missing her alleged killer's first court date.
The Reserve Bank of Australia has hiked interest rates to 4.35 per cent, marking the third consecutive rate increase, which is expected to bring new pain for would-be homebuyers.
The final stages of the inland rail project have been shelved due to a significant $45 billion cost blowout.

Meta is planning an advanced 'agentic' AI assistant for consumers and is facing a lawsuit from five large publishing groups over alleged copyright infringement for using copyrighted works to train its Llama AI models.
American Electric Power Co., a major US utility, is considering withdrawing from two of the country's largest electricity grids due to delays in connecting new AI data centers.
Alphabet Inc. is increasing its borrowing in the Euro debt market to fund significant investments in artificial intelligence.
